title: lathyrus latifolius subsp. ensifolius
intro: Lathyrus latifolius subsp. ensifolius, also known as broad-leaved peacock's ear or broad-leaved pea, is a flowering plant species in the pea family Fabaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and respiratory problems, offering relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus latifolius subsp. ensifolius can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
